title: Ambiguity in causal mask in scaled_dot_product_attention
issue_summary: PyTorch SDPA used a left-anchored lower-triangular causal mask even when query length and key length differed, which is the cached decoding case.

root_cause.summary: The causal mask was defined as tril(L, S) without offsetting query positions relative to the already cached key/value prefix.

symptom: A cached query can attend to too little history, and the first query in the continuation may see only one key when it should see the full prefix.

local_reproduction.mapping: The local case builds the buggy left-anchored mask and the corrected offset mask for L != S, then shows the difference in allowed history and output.

issue_contract.trigger: Run cached decoding with query length L smaller than key length S.
issue_contract.mechanism: The buggy path builds the causal mask without offsetting query positions into the cached prefix.
issue_contract.observable_symptom: The continuation query sees the wrong history and output values change.

reproduction_scope.mechanism_level_claim: This case reproduces the causal-mask offset error when query length differs from key length during cached decoding.
reproduction_scope.issue_level_observable_claim: This case reproduces the issue-observable symptom that cached queries see the wrong causal history and produce wrong outputs.
